subject: Breast
title: Duct Ectasia vs Periductal Mastitis
hook: A nipple mass in a smoker is not always cancer.

points.heading: Two look-alikes
bullets: Duct ectasia strikes multiparous women in their 50s to 60s
Periductal mastitis mainly strikes smokers, over 90% of cases
Smoking status is the exam's key differentiator

points.heading: Duct ectasia signs
bullets: Subareolar duct dilatation reaches 40% by age 70
Presents with nipple discharge, retraction or a doughy mass
Mass and retraction can mimic invasive carcinoma

points.heading: Periductal mastitis
bullets: Squamous metaplasia traps keratin until ducts rupture
Causes periareolar inflammation with purulent nipple discharge
Fistula classically opens at the areolar margin

points.heading: Do not miss this
bullets: Biopsy within triple assessment helps exclude malignancy
Bilateral green multiduct discharge is physiological, not pathological
Fistulas need excision, not simple fistulotomy

captionLines: A nipple mass in a smoker is not always cancer. 🩺
Duct ectasia hits multiparous women in their 50s and 60s while periductal mastitis targets smokers.
Smoking status is the exam's favourite way to split these two.
Recall Engine turns any lecture into SBAs, MCQs, flashcards and high-yield notes so you stop mixing up breast differentials under exam pressure.
